name: ForSeeBench
alternateName: ForSeeBench: Prospective Audio-Description QA for Evaluating Accessible Movie Understanding
description: Evaluation benchmark for prospective audio-description (AD) question answering. Each item gives prior AD context, a four-option multiple-choice question, and a withheld future human-written AD target. The task tests whether prior AD preserves evidence that supports inference about the next narrated visual update. Public rows omit the answer key; answer-bearing rows add scoring labels and clean audit metadata. The benchmark is derived from MAD-eval audio descriptions and contains 787 items across 10 movies. The release also includes the paper teaser and construction-pipeline figures. This release is an evaluation benchmark and is not intended as a training corpus.
citation: @misc{forseebench2026, title = {ForSeeBench: Prospective Audio-Description QA for Evaluating Accessible Movie Understanding}, author = {Anonymous Author(s)}, year = {2026}, howpublished = {NeurIPS 2026 Datasets and Benchmarks (under review)}, note = {Anonymous benchmark submission artifact}}
rai:dataLimitations: ForSeeBench is an evaluation benchmark, not a training corpus.

The benchmark is derived from movie audio-description data and should not be interpreted as complete accessibility evaluation; it does not measure overall accessibility quality, viewer satisfaction, or BLV comprehension, which require user-centered studies.

The release excludes raw movie videos, clips, audio tracks, subtitles, dialogue files, full MAD/MAD-eval assets, and other restricted source assets; users who need source assets must obtain them from the original providers under their own terms.
The benchmark targets prospective inference of the next human-narrated AD update, not long-horizon forecasting or full-scene planning.
rai:dataBiases: The source domain is professional movie audio description and may reflect movie-domain, genre, casting, and description-style biases.

Automated Qwen2.5-VL-assisted target/context selection and validation may favor examples whose textual evidence is explicit; visually salient but linguistically implicit cues are systematically harder to retain.
The 10 MAD-eval movies are not a uniform sample of cinema; results may not generalize to all film genres, languages, or AD styles.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: Derived text contains fictional character names and movie scene descriptions; no real-person personal or biometric information is included by construction.
The artifact is intended for benchmark review and should not be treated as a source of personal or biometric information.
rai:dataUseCases: Intended use: evaluating multiple-choice prospective QA from prior AD context.
Intended use: comparing AD context sources (Human MAD-eval AD, NarrAD, AutoAD-Zero) and context policies (no-context, fixed-window, adaptive) under fixed Q/A items.
Intended use: research on forward relevance of AD streams for accessibility-oriented video understanding.
Out-of-scope use: training on restricted source assets, raw media redistribution, accessibility certification, replacing user studies, or high-stakes deployment.
rai:dataSocialImpact: ForSeeBench supports evaluation of forward-relevant information in AD streams, with the goal of motivating AD systems that better serve blind and low-vision viewers. Risks include overclaiming accessibility impact or treating prospective QA accuracy as a substitute for blind/low-vision user studies. The benchmark deliberately excludes raw media to limit redistribution and surveillance risks.
rai:dataCollection: Source AD sentences are drawn from the 10-movie MAD-eval split. For each movie, ordered AD segments are converted into overlapping 10-clip search regions. Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct selects a hidden future target and supporting strictly-prior evidence clips, generates one four-option multiple-choice question with three typed distractors, and re-evaluates each candidate at validation time against three thresholds: Qwen confidence >= 0.7, evidence sufficiency >= 0.7, and distractor quality >= 0.7. Rejections are logged with inspectable reasons. ForSeeBench construction does not require manual writing of every item, although it derives from human-written MAD/MAD-eval AD.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Benchmark annotations are produced by Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct under strict-JSON prompts with stage-specific decoding settings. Selection, target extraction, evidence identification, and validation use temperature 0.1; question/answer-option generation uses temperature 0.4; the evaluation answerer uses temperature 0.0. Top-p is 0.8 and max_tokens is 2048 for construction stages unless overridden by the corresponding script. The exact prompts are reproduced in the paper appendix and in src/forseebench/qwen/prompts.py or the evaluation prompt builders.
rai:dataAnnotationPlatform: Local Hugging Face / vLLM inference of Qwen/Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct on a single-node GPU host. No third-party annotation platform was used.
rai:annotationsPerItem: One Qwen-generated annotation pass per benchmark item. Each item must additionally pass an independent Qwen-based validation pass (confidence, evidence sufficiency, distractor quality) before being retained.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: MAD-eval AD sentences are loaded as ordered c_1, ..., c_n per movie and converted into overlapping 10-clip candidate regions. The construction pipeline selects a hidden future target y and evidence from strictly prior clips i < y, validates evidence sufficiency and distractor quality, exports an answer-free public JSONL file and a matching answer-bearing scoring JSONL file, and keeps raw media/source assets out of the release.
rai:dataReleaseMaintenancePlan: Versioned releases are tagged on the Hugging Face dataset repository. Errata, corrections, optional paraphrasing of audited verbatim target-option cases, and any additional split definitions will be posted as new versions; the Croissant version field is bumped on every release. The current artifact is the anonymous review version.

prov:wasDerivedFrom.name: MAD / MAD-eval
prov:wasDerivedFrom.citation: Soldan et al., MAD: A Scalable Dataset for Language Grounding in Videos From Movie Audio Descriptions, CVPR 2022
prov:wasDerivedFrom.license: Original MAD/MAD-eval and underlying movie source assets remain governed by their original providers' terms and are not redistributed in this release.

distribution.name: qna_test.jsonl
distribution.description: Public no-answer benchmark file. One JSON object per line. 787 items.

distribution.name: qna_with_answers.jsonl
distribution.description: Answer-bearing scoring file. One JSON object per line. 787 items, each with the answer key and clean audit fields. Same item ids as qna_test.jsonl.

distribution.name: schema.md
distribution.description: Human-readable field-by-field schema for both JSONL files.

distribution.name: teaser.png
distribution.description: Paper teaser figure illustrating the prospective AD-QA setup: the answerer uses prior AD while the future Target AD is withheld.

distribution.name: data_pipeline.png
distribution.description: Construction pipeline figure showing source AD streams, target/evidence selection, MCQ generation, validation, and release files.

distribution.name: LICENSE.md
distribution.description: Review access terms for the ForSeeBench dataset artifact.

recordSet.name: public_examples
recordSet.description: One record per benchmark item in the public no-answer file. The answer key is intentionally absent.

field.name: id
field.description: Stable benchmark item id of the form lsmdc::<MOVIE>::block::<startidx>_<endidx>::w10::<target_clip_id>.

field.name: source_id
field.description: MAD-eval movie identifier (release-safe folder name).

field.name: prior_context
field.description: JSON array of prior AD clips visible to the answerer. Each element is an object with keys 'clip_id' (string) and 'text' (string). Encoded as JSON within a text field; consumers should parse it.

field.name: question
field.description: Multiple-choice question about the hidden future AD target.

field.name: options
field.description: JSON array of exactly four answer options (strings).

field.name: question_type
field.description: Controlled question type label (e.g., what_happens_next, what_is_revealed_next, what_changes_next, what_text_appears_next).

field.name: target_type
field.description: Controlled target type label: action_transition, state_change, participant_update, spatial_consequence, object_reveal, or visible_text_update.

field.name: context_length
field.description: Number of selected adaptive prior-context clips for this item.

field.name: expectedness
field.description: Optional Qwen-judged scalar in [0, 1] describing how strongly the prior context narrows the future event. Diagnostic; not used as a hard filter in the released benchmark.

recordSet.name: answer_bearing_examples
recordSet.description: One record per benchmark item in the answer-bearing scoring file. Item ids match public_examples; this file additionally provides the answer key, the hidden target AD, evidence spans, and distractor labels for evaluation and audit.

field.name: id
field.description: Stable benchmark item id; matches public_examples/id.

field.name: source_id
field.description: MAD-eval movie identifier.

field.name: prior_context
field.description: JSON array of prior AD clips. Elements are objects with keys 'clip_id' and 'text'. Same as public_examples/prior_context.

field.name: question
field.description: Multiple-choice question about the hidden future AD target.

field.name: options
field.description: JSON array of exactly four answer options (strings).

field.name: question_type
field.description: Controlled question type label.

field.name: target_type
field.description: Controlled target type label.

field.name: context_length
field.description: Number of selected adaptive prior-context clips.

field.name: expectedness
field.description: Optional Qwen-judged scalar in [0, 1].

field.name: answer_idx
field.description: Index of the correct option in `options`, in [0, 3].

field.name: answer_text
field.description: Text of the correct option; equal to options[answer_idx].

field.name: hidden_target_ad
field.description: Withheld future MAD-eval AD sentence used for scoring. Not shown to the answering model in any evaluation condition; provided only in the answer-bearing file for evaluation and reproducibility.

field.name: evidence
field.description: JSON array of evidence-span objects. Each element has keys 'clip_id' (string) and 'span' (string), where the span is an exact substring of a strictly-prior AD clip that supports the correct answer.

field.name: distractor_metadata
field.description: JSON array of length 4 aligned with `options`. One element is 'correct'; the other three are typed distractor labels: already_happened, entity_swapped, plausible_unsupported, contradicts_context, or unrelated.

rai:syntheticDataDescription: Questions, answer options, distractors, target/reasoning metadata, expectedness scores, and validation scores are produced by Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct under fixed prompts. Prior context and hidden target AD text are derived from MAD/MAD-eval human-written audio descriptions and are not synthetic.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: MAD-eval AD parsing and regioning
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Ordered MAD-eval AD clips are parsed per movie and converted into overlapping 10-clip candidate regions. Raw movie media, audio, subtitles, and full source assets are not redistributed.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Qwen target and evidence selection
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct selects a concrete hidden future AD target and strictly prior evidence-bearing context clips from each candidate region.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Qwen MCQ generation
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct generates one four-option multiple-choice question from the selected target and prior evidence, including a target-grounded correct option and typed distractors.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Validation and filtering
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Candidates are retained only when strict JSON validation succeeds and Qwen validation scores meet thresholds for answerability confidence, evidence sufficiency, and distractor quality.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Release export
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: The retained 787 items are exported into qna_test.jsonl without answers and qna_with_answers.jsonl with answer keys and audit fields.
